title: relbunium hypocarpium
side_effects: Relbunium hypocarpium can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach pain due to its toxic properties. Some users have reported symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and allergic reactions, particularly when ingested in large quantities or without proper preparation. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can lead to respiratory problems, such as bronchitis or asthma-like symptoms. If not handled carefully, relbunium hypocarpium can also cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering.
